title: Who Do I Become as the Start of the New Line
subtitle: A journal practice for Day 13 of the money arc
excerpt: A set of journal prompts for Day 13. The writing moves from what the new family money story sounds like, to what already reflects that story now, to who you become as the person who starts it.
sourceNotes: Prompt material from Day 13 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: On Day 13 of the money arc, or any morning when you want to write about what becoming the wealthy one in your family line actually means.

content.text: A set of journal prompts for Day 13. Write without editing — ten minutes against a timer if you want a container for it. The prompts move from the family story outward to who you specifically become as its starting point.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
If my family history began fresh with me, what would our money story sound like?
Write the story in plain terms — what changes, what stays, what the new version passes on.
What aspects of my current life already reflect this new story of prosperity?
Look for what's already different from the inherited pattern. Small things count.
Closer in
Who do I become as the start of a new line?
Name the woman specifically — what she does with money, how she talks about it, what she teaches without meaning to.
What is the single biggest thing I want to pass forward?
Not a value or a concept — a specific habit, a specific behaviour, a specific belief about money.
What would it look like if I fully believed I had already started this?
Write what today looks like from that position.
Where this practice comes from
Prompt material from Day 13 of
The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow
(Rebecca J Page, 2025).
